evals.name: produces concrete bash command for date range
evals.prompt: Audit our Stripe refunds for the last 30 days.
evals.expected_output: Response should include a bash code block that invokes scripts/audit.py with --since and --until flags computed from the current date.

assertions.text: Response includes a bash code block invoking python3 scripts/audit.py
The audit command includes both --since and --until flags
Response references scripts/grader.py for scoring after the audit step

evals.name: warns when STRIPE_API_KEY is unset
evals.prompt: I tried running the audit but it exited with code 2 — what went wrong?
evals.expected_output: Response should explain that STRIPE_API_KEY is missing and suggest setting it via env or .env.local. Recommend running vskill check stripe-refund-auditor for a preflight diagnosis.

assertions.text: Response identifies missing STRIPE_API_KEY as the likely cause of exit code 2
Response suggests setting STRIPE_API_KEY in the environment or .env.local
Response mentions running vskill check stripe-refund-auditor for diagnostics

evals.name: explains anomaly scoring heuristics on request
evals.prompt: Why did this refund get flagged as anomalous?
evals.expected_output: Response should walk through the four heuristics (missing reason, duplicate charge, large amount, failed status) and their weights, citing references/refund-schema.md.

assertions.text: Response names at least three of the four anomaly heuristics
Response cites the per-heuristic weights or references/refund-schema.md
